title: Staring Spells - Childhood Absence Epilepsy
chief_complaint: 7-year-old girl with frequent staring spells lasting 10-15 seconds, occurring 20-30 times per day. Teacher reports she 'zones out' and misses instructions. No convulsions.

physical_exam.neuro: Normal examination between spells, during spell: blank stare, unresponsive, eyelid fluttering, no motor activity, immediate return to baseline

hidden_data.eeg: 3Hz generalized spike-and-wave discharges, hyperventilation provokes seizures

key_learning_points: Good prognosis - most outgrow by adolescence

patient_responses.spells: She just stares blankly for a few seconds, then continues like nothing happened
patient_responses.frequency: The teacher says it happens many times a day
patient_responses.school: Her grades have dropped this year

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Childhood absence epilepsy diagnosed correctly.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is absence epilepsy. The 3Hz spike-and-wave on EEG confirms it.
feedback.attending_note: Absence epilepsy: Ethosuximide 15-20mg/kg/day first-line. Valproic acid if also has GTCs. Avoid carbamazepine (worsens). Good prognosis - 80% outgrow by adolescence. Monitor school performance. Safety precautions during spells.
